plan.what: Produce and validate the v0.1.0 distribution artifacts (source tarball and wheel) locally, without publishing anything yet.
plan.why: This is the step the previous cycle suggested, and it is the only part of the release path that needs no PyPI credentials. Validating that the package builds and installs cleanly from the wheel must happen before spending the project name on TestPyPI/PyPI, per the planning doc's publication process.
where: pyproject.toml (only if the build reveals a metadata problem)
plan.how: Install the standard tooling (build, twine), run 'python -m build' to produce the artifacts, run 'twine check dist/*' on the result, and then install the wheel into a fresh temporary virtual environment to prove it works outside this repo.
plan.expected_result: A dist/ directory containing stepgate-0.1.0.tar.gz and stepgate-0.1.0-py3-none-any.whl that pass twine's metadata check and install cleanly, leaving the project one token away from a TestPyPI upload.
plan.verification: twine check reports PASSED for both artifacts; in the temp venv, 'stepgate --version' prints 'stepgate 0.1.0' and 'stepgate init' works in a scratch directory.

data.summary: Built stepgate-0.1.0.tar.gz and .whl with python -m build; the first build shipped .stepgate/, AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md inside the sdist, so a sdist exclude list was added to pyproject.toml and the artifacts rebuilt

data.evidence: twine check: PASSED for both artifacts; wheel installed into a fresh venv: 'stepgate --version' printed 'stepgate 0.1.0' and 'stepgate init' created .stepgate/ in a scratch dir; sdist inspected: internal state files no longer packaged
